description: Your SOC needs to identify potential credential compromise by detecting 'Impossible Travel'—instances where a single user account successfully authenticates from multiple different countries. Using Splunk (http://localhost:8000, username: 'admin', password: 'SplunkAdmin1!'), analyze successful SSH authentications in the security_logs index to identify users authenticating from more than one distinct country. Save this detection logic as a report named 'Impossible_Travel_Detection'. Additionally, create a dashboard named 'Geo_Security_Monitoring' containing at least two panels, one of which must be a map visualization showing the geographic distribution of these authentications.
